codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of TopNavBar with fixed header, auth-aware user icon, search overlay integration, and comprehensive test coverage. Follows all project conventions (TypeScript strict, Tailwind CSS, lucide-react icons, next-auth, Next.js App Router). All 9 expectedBehavior items from the feature spec are satisfied. The component integrates properly into LayoutWrapper with conditional rendering for admin pages and correct padding offset. Z-index layering is consistent with BottomTabNavigation (both z-50) and SearchOverlay (z-60). 27 tests cover rendering, auth states, navigation, accessibility, touch targets, and visual feedback.

issues.description: useSession does not destructure or handle the 'status' property (loading/authenticated/unauthenticated). During initial session loading, 'data' is null so users briefly see the logged-out state (generic User icon linking to /login) even when authenticated. This causes a flash of unauthenticated content (FOUC). Consider showing a skeleton or neutral state while status === 'loading'. Non-blocking because the correct state resolves quickly and the UX is acceptable.

The useSession mock returns { data: null } for logged-out state, but the real useSession hook returns { data: null, status: 'unauthenticated' }. There's no test for the 'loading' state ({ data: null, status: 'loading' }). This means the loading state behavior is untested. Non-blocking since loading state handling was not explicitly required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md documents LayoutWrapper as wrapping 'SessionProvider, LazyMotion, ToastProvider, BottomTabNavigation' but does not mention TopNavBar, which is now also rendered inside LayoutWrapper with conditional visibility. The architecture doc should be updated to reflect the new layout structure including TopNavBar and Footer.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md line 'LayoutWrapper wraps all pages (SessionProvider, LazyMotion, ToastProvider, BottomTabNavigation)' — TopNavBar and Footer are now also rendered conditionally inside LayoutWrapper (see components/LayoutWrapper.tsx lines 24-29)

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md documents BottomTabNavigation hidden-on rules but does not document TopNavBar hidden-on rules. TopNavBar is hidden on /admin/* pages. This should be documented alongside the BottomTabNavigation visibility rules.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md says 'BottomTabNavigation hidden on: /login, /register, /admin/*, /products/*, /events/*' but has no mention of TopNavBar hidden on /admin/*

summary: PASS. The TopNavBar implementation is solid and meets all feature requirements. The component is well-structured with proper fixed positioning (z-50), auth-aware user icon via useSession, SearchOverlay integration, and correct LayoutWrapper integration with admin page exclusion. All touch targets meet the 44px minimum. 27 comprehensive tests pass. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) no handling of useSession loading state causing a brief flash of unauthenticated content, (2) no test for the loading state. Two shared state observations: architecture.md should be updated to document TopNavBar in the layout architecture and its visibility rules.
